references.notes: The verse refers to 'al-Manūn' (fate/death) as an uncontrollable cosmological force, a common pre-Islamic concept.
The phrase 'يكبر القدح العنود' refers to the practice of casting lots or divination arrows, a common pre-Islamic divinatory ritual.
The verse refers to 'al-Manāyā' (fates/deaths) as an inescapable cosmological force, a common pre-Islamic concept.
The verse mentions 'dahr' (time/fate) as a cosmological force that can betray or cause misfortune, reflecting a pre-Islamic belief.
The phrase 'laʿamruka' is a common pre-Islamic oath formula, swearing by the life of the addressee.
